input.title: Thymosin alpha-1 as adjunctive therapy in severe COVID-19: a case series of 12 patients
input.abstract: We report 12 consecutive patients with severe COVID-19 pneumonia (median age 64 years; 8 male) who received thymosin alpha-1 (1.6 mg subcutaneous, twice weekly) in addition to standard-of-care (dexamethasone + remdesivir) at a single centre between March and June 2021. Median duration of thymosin alpha-1 treatment was 14 days. Lymphocyte counts normalized within 7 days in 10 of 12 patients. Eight patients were discharged from intensive care within 21 days; 2 died of multi-organ failure (not attributed to thymosin alpha-1). No drug-related adverse events were observed. These observations suggest thymosin alpha-1 may support immune recovery in severe COVID-19, warranting controlled evaluation.
